architecture.description:
End-to-end data flow for cloud GPU training:
1. DATA: UpCloud S3 bucket "minivess-dvc-data" → dvc pull -r upcloud (on RunPod VM) → /app/data/raw/minivess/
2. COMPUTE: SkyPilot launches RunPod RTX 4090 spot instance → Clones repo, uv sync --all-extras → Native Python execution (NO Docker on RunPod) → MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1, PREFECT_DISABLED=1
3. TRAIN: train_flow.py → train_one_fold_task() → Pre-training checks (T2.1 diagnostics) → SegmentationTrainer.fit() with profiling (T1.1-T1.3) → WeightWatcher post-training (T2.2 diagnostics)
4. TRACK: MLflow → UpCloud VPS (http://IP:5000, basic auth) → PostgreSQL backend, S3 artifact storage → Resolved via resolve_tracking_uri() + MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env
5. VERIFY: Local machine queries UpCloud MLflow → Validate run status, params, metrics, artifacts → Verify profiling artifacts (Chrome traces, key_averages) → Verify diagnostics (pre-training checks, WeightWatcher)
No Docker registry needed. No Prefect server on cloud. Pulumi infrastructure provisioning is out of scope (assumed pre-existing).
architecture.no_docker_registry_rationale: SkyPilot smoke tests use native Python execution on RunPod VMs: - git clone the repo on the VM - uv sync --all-extras for dependencies - MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 bypasses _require_docker_context() guard - PREFECT_DISABLED=1 skips Prefect server requirement This is approved in CLAUDE.md for ephemeral cloud VMs (same as pytest). Docker-in-Docker on RunPod is unnecessary overhead for smoke tests.

hypothesis.title: VesselFM HuggingFace weight download fails on RunPod
hypothesis.description: VesselFM downloads vesselFM_base.pt from HF Hub (bwittmann/vesselFM). RunPod VMs have internet but HF may rate-limit or require HF_TOKEN. The model is public but large downloads can timeout on spot instances.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Verify HF_TOKEN is set in .env (passed to SkyPilot as env var) 2. smoke_test_gpu.yaml already includes HF_TOKEN passthrough 3. Pre-flight test: check HF repo accessibility before launch 4. Fallback: pretrained=false (random init) for infra testing only
hypothesis.test: T0.5 — Verify HF repo accessibility from local machine

hypothesis.title: DVC pull fails — data not pushed to UpCloud S3
hypothesis.description: DVC data must be PUSHED to UpCloud S3 before RunPod can pull it. If data was never pushed, dvc pull -r upcloud fails with missing files. Data is ~1 GB (211 files) — network transfer takes ~2 min.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Pre-flight: dvc status -r upcloud (verifies files exist remotely) 2. If not pushed: dvc push -r upcloud from local machine first 3. Test: T1.1 verifies DVC remote has all required files

hypothesis.title: UpCloud MLflow server unreachable from RunPod
hypothesis.description: MLflow on UpCloud VPS must be reachable from RunPod's network. Firewall rules, wrong port, or auth misconfiguration can block access.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Pre-flight: GET /health on MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI (no auth required) 2. Pre-flight: authenticated API call to verify credentials 3. UpCloud firewall must allow port 5000 from any IP 4. Existing test: test_cloud_mlflow.py::TestCloudMLflowHealth

hypothesis.title: VesselFM OOM on RTX 4090
hypothesis.description: VesselFM estimated ~10 GB VRAM for fine-tuning (batch=1). RTX 4090 has 24 GB — ample headroom. With profiling overhead, total should be ~11-12 GB. Very unlikely to OOM.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. CUDA memory stats (T4.1 prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb) will measure actual VRAM 2. If OOM: reduce patch_size or disable profiling as fallback 3. Post-run: update configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml with measured values

hypothesis.title: Pre-training checks crash with real VesselFM model
hypothesis.description: Our new diagnostics (T2.1-T2.3) have only been tested with mock models. VesselFM's binary-to-2-class conversion (cat[-logits, logits]) may confuse pre-training checks that expect standard 2-channel output. check_output_shape() expects model_config.out_channels=2, but VesselFM internally produces 1 channel then converts.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Local unit test with VesselFM adapter + pre-training checks (T2.1) 2. If check fails: adapt check to handle SegmentationOutput.logits 3. check_output_shape already handles hasattr(output, "logits") 4. Worst case: pre-check error aborts training → fix and re-run
hypothesis.test: T2.1 — Local VesselFM + pre-training check compatibility test

hypothesis.title: Hydra config composition fails with smoke_vesselfm
hypothesis.description: smoke_vesselfm.yaml may reference config groups that don't exist, or have incompatible overrides. compose_experiment_config() may fail if the config structure doesn't match what train_flow.py expects.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Local test: compose_experiment_config("smoke_vesselfm") succeeds 2. Verify all referenced config groups exist 3. Verify train_flow.py can consume the resolved config dict

hypothesis.title: SkyPilot RunPod launch fails — quota, API key, spot unavailable
hypothesis.description: RunPod spot instances may be unavailable, API key may be invalid, or $15 credit may be insufficient for the requested GPU. RTX 4090 spot on RunPod ≈ $0.44/hr.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Pre-flight: sky check runpod (validates API key + connectivity) 2. Pre-flight: check RunPod balance via API (if available) 3. Fallback: try RTX 3090 or A6000 (cheaper, still 24 GB) 4. Budget guard: max 3 attempts × $0.20 = $0.60 worst case

hypothesis.title: Git clone fails on RunPod — private repo or wrong branch
hypothesis.description: The repo may be private (requiring GitHub token) and the current branch feat/skypilot-runpod-gpu-offloading must be checked out to test the profiling code from this branch.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Verify repo is public OR GH_TOKEN is configured for SkyPilot 2. smoke_test_gpu.yaml must checkout the correct branch 3. Add git checkout feat/skypilot-runpod-gpu-offloading to setup 4. Or: push to main first, then test
hypothesis.test: T2.1 — Verify YAML setup section handles branch checkout

hypothesis.title: Profiling artifacts too large for MLflow upload
hypothesis.description: Chrome traces can be 50+ MB. Gzip compression (T1.2) should reduce to ~5 MB, but MLflow + S3 upload may still be slow or timeout. The 50 MB size gate should prevent oversized uploads.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. TraceHandler has compress_traces=True and trace_size_limit_mb=50 2. Only 2 epochs profiled → small traces 3. Post-run: verify profiling/ artifact path has traces
hypothesis.test: T4.4 — Verify profiling artifacts exist in MLflow

hypothesis.title: WeightWatcher fails on VesselFM's DynUNet backbone
hypothesis.description: WeightWatcher analyzes 2D conv + linear layers only (not 3D conv). VesselFM uses MONAI DynUNet which has 3D convolutions. WeightWatcher may return 0 layers analyzed.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. run_weightwatcher() already handles 0 layers (returns NaN alpha) 2. VesselFM DynUNet may have some 2D projection layers 3. Post-run: check diag_ww_num_layers_analyzed in MLflow metrics 4. If 0: this is expected behavior, not a bug — document it
hypothesis.test: T4.5 — Verify WeightWatcher metrics logged (even if NaN)

hypothesis.title: Splits file smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json doesn't exist
hypothesis.description: smoke_vesselfm.yaml references splits_file: configs/splits/smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json. This file may not exist or may have wrong volume IDs.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. Pre-flight: verify file exists locally 2. If missing: create it with 2 train + 2 val volumes from MiniVess 3. Verify volume IDs match the data on UpCloud S3

hypothesis.title: uv sync fails on RunPod due to CUDA mismatch
hypothesis.description: RunPod RTX 4090 instances typically run CUDA 12.x. PyTorch wheels must match the CUDA version on the VM. uv sync may install CPU-only PyTorch if CUDA not detected.
hypothesis.mitigation: 1. smoke_test_gpu.yaml setup section should verify nvidia-smi 2. PyTorch index URL may need --extra-index-url for CUDA wheels 3. Existing smoke tests have already validated this path
hypothesis.test: Verified by existing smoke test infrastructure

phase.name: Pre-Flight Validation
phase.description: All checks run locally. Zero GPU cost. Must all pass before spending RunPod credits. Reuses existing validate_smoke_test_env.py.

task.title: Verify all required env vars are set
task.issue_title: test(cloud): pre-flight env var validation for VesselFM smoke test
task.description: Run scripts/validate_smoke_test_env.py to check: - DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY, DVC_S3_BUCKET - RUNPOD_API_KEY - MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI, MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME, MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD - HF_TOKEN (for VesselFM pretrained weight download) Also verify .env file has these values (not just empty strings).

tdd_spec.green: Add HF_TOKEN to required vars list in validate_smoke_test_env.py. Create test_preflight_validation.py wrapping the validate script.

task.title: Test MLflow cloud health + authentication
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify MLflow cloud server health and auth
task.description: Verify UpCloud MLflow server is reachable and authenticates correctly. Reuses existing tests/v2/cloud/test_cloud_mlflow.py::TestCloudMLflowHealth. Run: make test-cloud-mlflow (subset: health tests only).

tdd_spec.red: Run existing health tests — they should pass if server is up.
tdd_spec.green: No new code needed if existing tests pass.

task.title: Test DVC S3 connectivity to UpCloud
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify DVC remote connectivity to UpCloud S3
task.description: Verify DVC can reach UpCloud Object Storage with configured credentials. Reuses existing tests/v2/cloud/test_dvc_cloud_pull.py. Also verify: dvc status -r upcloud shows files exist.

tdd_spec.green: No new code if tests pass. If data not pushed, go to T1.1.

task.title: Test SkyPilot RunPod backend availability
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify SkyPilot RunPod provider is configured
task.description: Run: sky check runpod Verify API key is valid and RunPod backend is available. Check RTX 4090 spot availability and pricing.

tdd_spec.red: sky check runpod must return success.
tdd_spec.green: No new code — existing validate script covers this.

task.title: Verify HuggingFace repo accessibility
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify VesselFM HF weight download path
task.description: Check that bwittmann/vesselFM is accessible from current machine. Do NOT download the full weights — just verify repo exists via HF API. Verify HF_TOKEN auth works if repo requires it.

tdd_spec.green: Use huggingface_hub.repo_info() to verify repo metadata. Check "vesselFM_base.pt" is in the file list.

task.title: Verify Hydra config composition for smoke_vesselfm
task.issue_title: test(config): verify smoke_vesselfm experiment config composes
task.description: Test that compose_experiment_config("smoke_vesselfm") produces a valid config dict with expected keys: model_family=vesselfm, max_epochs=2, num_folds=1, debug=True, pretrained=True in architecture_params. Also verify the resolved config can be consumed by train_flow.py.

tdd_spec.green: Use compose_experiment_config("smoke_vesselfm") and assert on resolved dict. Fix smoke_vesselfm.yaml if any fields are missing or wrong.

phase.name: Data Preparation
phase.description: Ensure MiniVess data is on UpCloud S3 and splits file exists. One-time setup — subsequent runs skip this phase.

task.title: Push MiniVess data to UpCloud S3 via DVC
task.issue_title: chore(data): ensure MiniVess data pushed to UpCloud S3
task.description: If dvc status -r upcloud shows missing files: 1. Run: uv run python scripts/configure_dvc_remote.py 2. Run: dvc push -r upcloud 3. Verify: dvc status -r upcloud shows "Data and calculation files are up to date" This is a one-time operation (~1 GB upload).

tdd_spec.red: dvc status -r upcloud shows missing files.
tdd_spec.green: dvc push -r upcloud uploads all 211 files.

task.title: Verify smoke test splits file exists
task.issue_title: test(config): verify smoke test 1-fold splits file
task.description: smoke_vesselfm.yaml references splits_file: configs/splits/smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json. Verify this file exists, has 1 fold, 2 train + 2 val volumes, and volume IDs match actual MiniVess data files. If it doesn't exist: create it from the main 3-fold split.

tdd_spec.green: Read the splits file, validate structure. If missing: create with first 2 train + 2 val from fold 0.

phase.name: Local Compatibility Tests
phase.description: Test that our new profiling/diagnostics code works with VesselFM before spending GPU credits. Run on CPU with model_loading marker.

task.title: VesselFM + diagnostics compatibility test
task.issue_title: test(diagnostics): verify pre-training checks work with VesselFM
task.description:
Test that run_pre_training_checks() works with VesselFM adapter: - check_output_shape: VesselFM binary→2-class conversion - check_gradient_flow: at least one param has gradients - check_nan_inf: no NaN/Inf in output - check_loss_sanity: loss at init is finite
Also test run_weightwatcher() with VesselFM (may return 0 layers since DynUNet uses 3D conv, which WeightWatcher doesn't analyze).
This catches H5 and H10 before spending GPU credits.

tdd_spec.green: Build VesselFM adapter (pretrained=False for speed). Create sample batch (1,1,64,64,16). Run each diagnostic function and verify no crash.

task.title: Verify SkyPilot YAML handles VesselFM + branch checkout
task.issue_title: test(skypilot): verify smoke_test_gpu.yaml supports VesselFM model
task.description:
Verify smoke_test_gpu.yaml: 1. MODEL_FAMILY env var accepts "vesselfm" 2. Setup section clones correct branch (or main) 3. EXPERIMENT env var is set correctly for VesselFM 4. HF_TOKEN is passed to the VM environment 5. Profiling config is included (our new branch code)
Add branch checkout to setup if not present.

red.test: test_yaml_has_branch_checkout — git checkout for feature branch

tdd_spec.green: Parse smoke_test_gpu.yaml and validate env vars + setup section. Add branch checkout if missing. Add HF_TOKEN if missing.

phase.name: GPU Execution on RunPod
phase.description: Launch VesselFM training on RunPod RTX 4090 via SkyPilot. Monitor job status. This is the actual GPU spend. Expected duration: 15-20 minutes (setup + 2 epochs + teardown).

task.title: Launch SkyPilot VesselFM smoke test job
task.issue_title: feat(smoke-test): launch VesselFM training on RunPod via SkyPilot
task.description:
Execute: make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=vesselfm Which runs: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml -e MODEL_FAMILY=vesselfm -y
Monitor with: sky jobs queue Expected: RUNNING → SUCCEEDED in 15-20 min. Cost: ~$0.20 (RTX 4090 spot × 20 min).

tdd_spec.green: sky jobs launch succeeds, job enters RUNNING state.

task.title: Monitor job and collect logs
task.issue_title: chore(smoke-test): monitor SkyPilot job status
task.description: Poll job status with: sky jobs queue Collect logs with: sky jobs logs JOB_ID Expected terminal states: SUCCEEDED or FAILED. If FAILED: read logs, diagnose, iterate.

tdd_spec.green: Job reaches SUCCEEDED status.

phase.name: Post-Run Artifact Verification
phase.description: Query UpCloud MLflow from local machine to verify all artifacts, metrics, params, and diagnostics were correctly logged. This is the REAL integration test — validates the entire pipeline. These tests become the permanent cloud test suite.

task.title: Verify MLflow run exists with correct status
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify VesselFM smoke test run on cloud MLflow
task.description: Query UpCloud MLflow for the smoke test experiment. Verify: - At least one FINISHED run exists - Run has model_family=vesselfm tag - Run has flow_name tag - Run duration is reasonable (5-20 min) Extends existing test_cloud_training_artifacts.py.

tdd_spec.green: Use _find_latest_smoke_run("vesselfm") to locate the run. Assert FINISHED status and correct tags.

task.title: Verify training params and metrics logged
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify VesselFM smoke test params and metrics
task.description: Verify the MLflow run has: Params: loss_name, model_family, max_epochs, batch_size, arch_* Metrics: train_loss (at epoch 0 and 1), val metrics (if validation ran) System: sys_python_version, sys_gpu_model, sys_cuda_version

tdd_spec.green: Query run params and metrics via MlflowClient. Assert expected keys are present with non-empty values.

task.title: Verify profiling artifacts from this branch
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify profiling artifacts (Chrome traces, key_averages)
task.description: Verify our new profiling code (T0.1-T1.3) produced artifacts: - profiling/summary.json exists in MLflow artifacts - profiling/key_averages.txt exists - profiling/*.json.gz (Chrome traces) if export_chrome_trace=True - prof_overhead_pct metric logged - prof_trace_size_mb metric logged - prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb metric logged (T4.1 CUDA memory stats)

tdd_spec.green: List artifacts at profiling/ path via MlflowClient. Query prof_* metrics from run.

task.title: Verify diagnostics artifacts (pre-checks + WeightWatcher)
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify diagnostics ran on cloud training
task.description: Verify our diagnostics (T2.1-T2.3) ran: - Pre-training checks: all passed (no severity=error → training ran) - WeightWatcher: diag_ww_* metrics logged (even if num_layers=0) - Diagnostics ran unconditionally (RC17)

tdd_spec.green: Query diag_ww_* metrics from the smoke test run. If training completed, pre-checks must have passed (they abort on error).

task.title: Verify Hydra config artifact logged
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify resolved Hydra config artifact on cloud MLflow
task.description:
Verify config/resolved_config.yaml artifact exists and contains: - model_family: vesselfm - max_epochs: 2 - debug: true - experiment_name matching smoke_vesselfm pattern
This verifies the Hydra-zen → MLflow artifact pipeline (CLAUDE.md Rule #23).

tdd_spec.green: Download config/resolved_config.yaml via MlflowClient. Parse YAML and assert expected fields.

task.title: Verify GPU benchmark cache integration
task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify sys_bench_* params from GPU benchmark
task.description:
Verify our GPU benchmark cache (T3.1-T3.3) logged sys_bench_* params: - sys_bench_gpu_model (normalized GPU name, e.g., "rtx_4090") - sys_bench_total_vram_mb - sys_bench_driver_version
Note: benchmark cache may not exist on RunPod (first run). If missing: verify warning was logged but training continued (non-blocking).

red.test: test_gpu_benchmark_params_or_warning — sys_bench_* present OR cache missing OK

tdd_spec.green: Query sys_bench_* params. If absent, verify training still completed. GPU benchmark cache is non-blocking (warning, not error).

task.title: Update VesselFM VRAM profile with measured values
task.issue_title: docs(model-profiles): update VesselFM VRAM with RTX 4090 measurements
task.description: Read prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb from the smoke test run. Update configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml with MEASURED values (replacing "~10 GB estimated" with actual numbers). Update MEMORY.md VRAM table.

tdd_spec.green: VRAM values updated with measured prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb.

phase.name: Regression Baseline Establishment
phase.description: Use the verified smoke test run to establish the first performance baseline for regression detection (T4.2). Future runs can compare against this baseline using detect_regression().

task.title: Record baseline metrics for regression detection
task.issue_title: feat(diagnostics): establish VesselFM RTX 4090 performance baseline
task.description:
Extract from the successful smoke test run: - prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb (VRAM baseline) - prof_overhead_pct (profiler overhead baseline) - train_loss at final epoch (convergence baseline) - Total training time (speed baseline)
Store as the first data point for get_profiling_baseline(). The sys_bench_gpu_model_normalized tag enables GPU-specific baselines.

tdd_spec.green: First run provides baseline. count=1 after this run.

budget_for_iterations.note: With $0.15-$0.20 per run, we can afford 75+ iterations. Even with 5 failed attempts before success, total cost is ~$1.00. Budget is extremely comfortable for this testing.

fallback_gpu.name: RTX 3090 (24 GB)
fallback_gpu.note: Cheaper alternative if RTX 4090 spot unavailable.

reuse_map.description: ZERO redundant code. Every component reuses existing infrastructure.

existing_component.name: deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml
existing_component.purpose: SkyPilot task for RunPod GPU training
existing_component.modification: Minor: add HF_TOKEN, branch checkout if needed

existing_component.name: configs/experiment/smoke_vesselfm.yaml
existing_component.purpose: Hydra experiment config for VesselFM debug

existing_component.name: tests/v2/cloud/conftest.py
existing_component.purpose: Cloud test fixtures (connection, cleanup, S3 client)

execution_order.step: Phase 0: Pre-flight validation (T0.1-T0.6) — ALL must pass
Phase 2: Local compatibility (T2.1-T2.2) — catch bugs before GPU spend
Phase 3: GPU execution (T3.1-T3.2) — THE actual RunPod run
Phase 5: Baseline establishment (T5.1) — regression detection seed

implementation_notes.note: All test implementation follows the self-learning-iterative-coder TDD skill. RED → GREEN → VERIFY → FIX → CHECKPOINT → CONVERGE for each task.
Cloud tests (Phase 0, 4) auto-skip if MLFLOW_CLOUD_* env vars are not set. This makes them safe to include in the standard test suite.
Phase 3 (GPU execution) is manual — triggered by `make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=vesselfm`. It is NOT automated in CI (CLAUDE.md: GitHub Actions CI EXPLICITLY DISABLED).
The profiling code from this branch (T0.1-T4.2) has NEVER been tested with real GPU training. This is the first real-data validation. Expect glitches.
VesselFM was pre-trained on MiniVess → data leakage. This smoke test validates INFRASTRUCTURE, not scientific quality. The data_leakage tag must be logged.

The test suite is designed for production reuse: any model (sam3_vanilla, dynunet, vesselfm) can be tested with: make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=<name>. Verification tests are parameterizable by model family.

appendix.title: Verbatim User Prompt (2026-03-14)
appendix.content: So we can next create a plan for a script creation and running a the debug test (same as benchmarking/profiling the Runpod with 4090) to /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/runpod-debug-profiling.xml which will finetune first vesselfm with our debug dataset (2 train, 2 validation volumes) for 2 epochs using the 1 fold. The test should demonstrate that artifacts go to the MLflow server on UpCloud, the training is handled via Skypilot, and that the data can be obtained from the DVC versioned dataset in some "S3 simulation" location. I have $15 credits on Runpod so this testing should not consume those all. You should obviously have then the readout from the MLflow and its artifact store to validate that the MLflow registry work, do not cheat at all here. This is the integration test for those. There should be ZERO redundant code created, do deep exploration what your config system via Hydra is, how the Prefect Flows work and the Dockers need to be pulled by the Runpod (do we need to implement Dockerhub registry?). Do deep analysis with multiple rounds of reviewer agents coming up with how to test all our created code implemented in this branch! We have not yet tested this with real data using our real production code so there are probably a lot of glitches. Try to do multi-hypothesis preparation for all sorts of problems that we might be encountering. Remember use existing code! This is not some separate test for "dev" branch, but this should be the actual test suite that we will be using also in production to test that the UpCloud MLflow - Skypilot - Runpod GPU and DVC data versioning work together, with or without Pulumi (this is probably outside of the scope as we assume that the UpCloud instance spinning is taken care by some other test then). Think the whole test suite needed around this to run this testing. Implementation of anything in the .xml plan obviously via our self-learning TDD Skill! Save first my prompt as verbatim to the appendix of the .xml and let's start this!
